fault_hypothesis: The bug is on line 68. The strict inequality `now > effective` fails to consider an entry expired when `now == effective`, violating the requirement that entries are valid only when `now` is strictly before the expiry time.

constraints.intent: The comparison at this line is flawed for the boundary case where the current time equals the expiry time.
The cached value should only be returned if the current time is strictly before the expiry time.

spec.expr: (key not in entries) or (now < entries[key][1]) or (__cbfl_result is None)

constraints.intent: An entry should be returned only if it exists and is not expired.
The effective expiry time is expected to be identical to the stored expiry timestamp.
